# Agent Kai

> GTM engineering as tested code. Agent Kai runs a tested, open-source GTM engine on your CRM to recover the revenue trapped in broken pipeline data — on your own infrastructure, with zero data egress.

Agent Kai is a solo GTM-engineering firm. The flagship offer, Pipeline Recovery, finds dead pipeline, dedupes and re-enriches CRM data, repairs lead routing, and re-ranks accounts by recoverable revenue. It runs on a public, MIT-licensed decision engine (kaikarma-gtm-engine, 774 passing tests) executed on the client's own infrastructure, so customer data never leaves their VPC. Ideal for US/EU B2B SaaS teams (≈50–1000 employees) on HubSpot or Salesforce with a rotted CRM.

## Offer

- Pipeline Audit: $3,000 fixed, 2 weeks. A quantified recovery report — how much pipeline is recoverable, where it leaks, ranked by dollars. The fee is credited into month one.
- Pipeline Recovery retainer: $10,000/month, month-to-month. Clean, enrich, re-route, reactivate dormant accounts, and report recovered pipeline monthly. Cancel anytime.
- Guarantee: if the audit doesn't surface at least 10× its fee in recoverable pipeline, it's free.

## How it works

- Score accounts as a tested function (firmographic, technographic, signal, fit) — not a 100-point spreadsheet.
- Control paid spend under hard caps; route leads by data-quality score and SLA.
- Learn from closed-won/lost outcomes and re-rank the pipeline.
- Runs offline, deterministic, on the client's infrastructure — zero data egress. Auditable, tested code rather than a black-box tool.

## Differentiators

- Open-source and tested: the decision engine is public, MIT-licensed, 774 passing tests.
- Zero data egress: runs on your infrastructure; customer data never goes to a third-party vendor.
- Revenue, not hygiene: every engagement is measured in recovered pipeline dollars, not dedupe counts.

## Links

- [Open-source GTM engine on GitHub](https://github.com/Kai8karma/kaikarma-gtm-engine): the MIT-licensed decision engine, 774 tests, that powers every engagement.
- [Book a pipeline audit](mailto:kai8karma@gmail.com): start with a two-week, quantified audit.
